commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(agents): route exec approval turn source to messageChannel
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Exec-tool approval requests resolved the turn-source channel from `messageProvider` only:

```ts
turnSourceChannel: defaults?.messageProvider,
```

Everywhere else, the canonical turn-source channel is `messageChannel ?? messageProvider` (`agent-tools.ts`: `const turnSourceChannel = options?.messageChannel ?? options?.messageProvider`; `agent-command.ts`: `turnSourceChannel: opts.runContext?.messageChannel ?? opts.messageChannel`). `messageChannel` is documented as the *"canonical transport channel when the tool-policy provider differs from the delivery channel"* — so when a session's delivery channel differs from its tool-policy provider, the exec approval path dropped the canonical channel and routed the approval (and its approved-followup delivery) to the wrong channel.

`ExecToolDefaults` did not even carry `messageChannel`, so the channel was lost when `agent-tools.ts` built the exec defaults.

## Why This Change Was Made
Mirror the canonical resolution at the exec approval boundary, without disturbing the other `messageProvider` uses (tool availability, event routing) that legitimately want the provider:
- add `messageChannel?` to `ExecToolDefaults`;
- forward `messageChannel: options?.messageChannel` where `agent-tools.ts` builds the exec defaults;
- resolve `turnSourceChannel: defaults?.messageChannel ?? defaults?.messageProvider` at both exec approval sites.

When `messageChannel` is unset (the common case) behavior is unchanged — it falls back to `messageProvider`. Production delta is 3 lines across 3 files; the rest is a regression test.

## User Impact
For sessions where the delivery channel differs from the tool-policy provider, an exec approval prompt and its approved-followup delivery now route to the correct canonical channel instead of the tool-policy provider.

## Evidence

New regression test in `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts` drives the real `createExecTool` gateway approval flow with `messageChannel: "feishu"` and `messageProvider: "internal"`, and asserts the approved followup delivery routes to `channel: "feishu"` (the turn-source channel), not `"internal"`.

```text
# WITH FIX
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts \
    --run -t "routes the approval turn source"
exit 0  (test passes — followup channel === "feishu")

# NEGATIVE CONTROL — revert only bash-tools.exec.ts to origin/main (keep the test)
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts \
    --run -t "routes the approval turn source"
exit 1  (test fails — followup channel falls back to messageProvider "internal")
```

This proves the bug is real (the unfixed resolution drops `messageChannel`) and the test guards it.

**No regression — full approval suite + lint:**
```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts --run
exit 0  (all tests pass)

$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-types.ts \
    src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts
exit 0  (clean)
```

Production delta: `agent-tools.ts` +1, `bash-tools.exec-types.ts` +3, `bash-tools.exec.ts` +4/-2.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
